LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFFS HAILU ANTENEH, RANDALL FELICIANO, JOSEPH TRACY, IRA NEWMAN, JOHN (MI) COLEMAN, CAROL GAHAGAN, JOHN KERR, ALFRED MCLEAN, RICHARD GRAHAM, ARMAN SHIRVANI AND GARTH CHOJNOWSKI'S MOTION FOR ENFORCEMENT OF JUDGMENT IS GRANTED IN PART. ALL OTHER RELIEF SOUGHT BY THIS MOTION IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO PLAINTIFFS SEEKING SUCH RELIEF BY WAY OF AN APPLICATION FOR CONTEMPT. ON ITS OWN MOTION THE COURT REOPENS DISCOVERY FOR BOTH PLAINTIFFS AND LUXOR WITH A DISCOVERY CUTOFF DATE OF DECEMBER 31, 2017. ATTORNEY'S FEES ARE RECOVERABLE IN A CONTEMPT PROCEEDING PER CCP 1218(A), BUT NOT ON THIS MOTION. THE COURT CANNOT AWARD FEES UNDER ITS INHERENT POWERS PER CCP 128 AND 187. (CLARK V. OPTICAL COATING LABORATORY, INC. (2008) 165 CAL.APP.4TH 150, 164-166.) ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA. REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR #5976. (302/HK)

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion For Enforcement Of Judgment
Off Calendar Dec-08-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

The Court confirmed its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFFS HAILU ANTENEH, RANDALL FELICIANO, JOSEPH TRACY, IRA NEWMAN, JOHN (MI) COLEMAN, CAROL GAHAGAN, JOHN KERR, ALFRED MCLEAN, RICHARD GRAHAM, ARMAN SHIRVANI AND GARTH CHOJNOWSKI'S Motion For Enforcement Of Judgment is granted in part. Defendant Luxor Cabs, Inc. is admonished that the injunctive relief provisions of the February 11, 1997 judgment, including the provisions regarding unemployment insurance benefits, remain fully binding as to it. All other relief sought by this motion is denied without prejudice to plaintiffs seeking such relief by way of an application for contempt. On its own motion the court reopens discovery for both plaintiffs and Luxor with a discovery cutoff date of December 31, 2017. While the court appreciates plaintiffs' efforts to be efficient by not invoking contempt procedures, absent a stipulation, contempt is the proper means for most of the relief sought by plaintiffs, including plaintiffs' request for attorney's fees. Attorney's fees are recoverable in a contempt proceeding per CCP 1218(a), but not on this motion. The court cannot award fees under its inherent powers per CCP 128 and 187. (Clark v. Optical Coating Laboratory, Inc. (2008) 165 Cal.App.4th 150, 164-166.) Order signed.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)
